selected3.name: Budget Planner App - Fleur
selected3.prd:
## Product Summary
Mobile-first budgeting assistant for everyday consumers to set category limits, log spending quickly, and receive proactive overspend alerts.

## User Problem
People know they should budget but quit complex finance tools. They need a fast, low-friction routine they can keep.

## Target Users
- Young professionals controlling discretionary spend
- Students with fixed monthly constraints
- Households trying to cut avoidable expenses

## JTBD
- Set a practical budget in minutes
- Track daily purchases with minimal effort
- See early warning before month-end overspend
- Adjust categories fast when life changes

## MVP Scope
- Onboarding with income + fixed bills + starter categories
- Category budget allocation and monthly reset
- Manual expense logging + recurring transactions
- Dashboard: remaining budget + safe-to-spend/day
- Alerts: threshold and forecast nudges
- Weekly insight cards

## Out of Scope
- Bank integrations in v1
- Credit score/investing modules
- Multi-user shared ledger

## UX Principles
- 10-second expense logging
- One-screen daily status
- Clear, non-judgmental language

## Monetization
- Free: core budgeting + limited history
- Premium: unlimited history, custom rules, advanced forecast

## Success Metrics
- Setup completion >60%
- W2 retention >30%
- 6+ logs/WAU
- Premium conversion >3%

## Risks & Mitigations
- Entry fatigue -> recurring templates + shortcuts
- Churn after setup -> weekly progress nudges
- Crowded category -> superior speed and clarity

## Build Plan
- Week 1: onboarding + budgets + logging
- Week 2: dashboard + alerts + forecast
- Week 3: paywall + polish + analytics

selected3.name: Water tracker Waterllama
selected3.prd:
## Product Summary
Friendly hydration tracker that helps users maintain daily water goals with reminders and simple progress visuals.

## User Problem
Users forget to hydrate during busy days and generic reminders feel annoying or easy to ignore.

## Target Users
- Office workers and students
- Fitness and wellness users
- Users with recurring dehydration patterns

## JTBD
- Know my hydration target for today
- Log intake in one tap
- Receive reminders at useful times

## MVP Scope
- Personalized daily target
- Quick-add intake presets
- Smart reminders by schedule
- Streaks + simple trends
- Widget support

## Out of Scope
- Wearable integrations in v1
- Medical-grade dehydration diagnostics

## UX Principles
- Playful but lightweight
- One-handed, one-tap interactions

## Monetization
- Free: core tracking
- Premium: advanced analytics, custom reminder logic, themes

## Success Metrics
- D7 retention >35%
- 4+ logs/day active users
- Premium conversion >2.5%

## Risks & Mitigations
- Reminder fatigue -> adaptive reminder spacing
- Commodity risk -> better habit design + delight

## Build Plan
- Week 1: core tracking + reminders
- Week 2: trends + widgets
- Week 3: premium + polish

selected3.name: Packing List Checklist
selected3.prd:
## Product Summary
Trip packing assistant that auto-builds editable checklists based on destination, weather, and trip type.

## User Problem
Users forget essentials or overpack because planning starts late and generic lists are not contextual.

## Target Users
- Frequent travelers
- Families planning holidays
- Business travelers needing repeat templates

## JTBD
- Generate trip-ready packing list quickly
- Reuse and customize proven templates
- Avoid forgetting essentials before departure

## MVP Scope
- Trip profile: destination, dates, trip type
- Auto-generated base checklist
- Editable categories and item quantities
- Progress tracking + pre-departure reminders
- Reusable templates

## Out of Scope
- Booking/itinerary integrations in v1
- Team travel collaboration

## UX Principles
- Checklist-first interface
- Fast edit and duplicate actions

## Monetization
- Free: basic trips and lists
- Premium: unlimited templates, smart suggestions, family mode

## Success Metrics
- First-trip completion >70%
- Repeat trip creation >40% in 30 days
- Premium conversion >2%

## Risks & Mitigations
- Seasonal usage -> template ecosystem + reminders
- Low differentiation -> context-aware generation + speed

## Build Plan
- Week 1: trip model + checklist engine
- Week 2: reminders + templates
- Week 3: premium features + polish

selected3.name: Cursor AI Mobile - Remote IDE
selected3.prd:
# Product Requirements Document — Cursor AI Mobile - Remote IDE

## 1) Product Summary
A mobile companion app for developers who need to monitor and operate remote coding sessions from iPhone without opening a laptop. The app focuses on fast incident response, command execution, and context continuity.

## 2) User Problem
Developers miss critical windows because desktop tools are not practical in transit. Existing terminal apps are generic, require too much manual setup, and do not preserve coding context well.

## 3) Target Users
- Solo founders shipping continuously
- On-call engineers in small teams
- Indie hackers managing multiple services

## 4) Core Jobs-to-be-Done
- Check if production is healthy in under 30 seconds
- Run trusted quick actions (restart, flush cache, inspect logs)
- Continue async coding context while away from desktop

## 5) MVP Scope
### 5.1 Host & Project Management
- Add/edit/remove hosts
- Associate project profile with each host
- Connectivity status and last seen

### 5.2 Quick Action Console
- Predefined commands with confirmation
- Command output stream view
- Failure/success state with timestamps

### 5.3 Session Context
- Prompt history and command history
- Lightweight note thread per project
- Bookmark frequent actions

### 5.4 Alerts
- Push notification on command completion/failure
- Optional “critical only” mode

### 5.5 Security Baseline
- Token-based auth
- Device-level lock for sensitive actions
- Audit trail for all executed actions

## 6) Out of Scope (MVP)
- Full IDE editing experience
- Multi-user permission matrix
- Advanced CI/CD orchestration

## 7) UX Principles
- Two taps to any critical action
- Readability first in output logs
- Clear destructive action confirmations

## 8) Monetization
- Free: 1 host, basic actions, limited history
- Pro Monthly: multi-host, unlimited history, advanced alerts
- Team (later): shared runbooks and role permissions

## 9) Success Metrics
- Activation: first host connected within 10 minutes
- D7 retention: >25%
- Weekly action depth: 3+ actions/user
- Conversion: free→pro >4%

## 10) Risks & Mitigations
- Security trust risk → transparent audit logs + strict confirmations
- Infra variance risk → host compatibility checklist
- Market competition → mobile-first speed and UX wedge

## 11) Build Plan
- Week 1: Host model, quick actions, command logs
- Week 2: alerts, history sync, polish
- Week 3: onboarding improvements + paywall test

selected3.name: Clouder for Cloudflare
selected3.prd:
# Product Requirements Document — Clouder for Cloudflare

## 1) Product Summary
A mobile operations layer for Cloudflare users to perform critical infrastructure tasks quickly from phone, with a focus on DNS, cache, workers status, and incident visibility.

## 2) User Problem
Cloudflare dashboard is powerful but not optimized for rapid, high-confidence mobile workflows. During incidents, users need fewer screens and safer one-tap operations.

## 3) Target Users
- Indie founders managing traffic/security themselves
- Agencies handling multiple client zones
- Small devops teams that need emergency controls

## 4) Core Jobs-to-be-Done
- Validate zone health instantly
- Execute critical controls (DNS hotfix, cache purge)
- Observe workers/pages status without desktop friction

## 5) MVP Scope
### 5.1 Account & Zone Layer
- Multi-account switch
- Zone list with status chips
- Favorites pinning

### 5.2 DNS Control
- Record list/search
- Add/edit/delete records with validation
- Rollback recent DNS changes

### 5.3 Cache & Edge Actions
- One-click cache purge (global/selective)
- Confirmation step for destructive actions

### 5.4 Workers / Pages Visibility
- Deployment status feed
- Last error snapshot
- Quick links to logs

### 5.5 Alerts & Timeline
- Incident timeline view
- Push alerts for critical events

## 6) Out of Scope (MVP)
- Full WAF rule builder
- Complex automation pipelines
- Full analytics suite parity with desktop

## 7) UX Principles
- “Calm under pressure” design
- Action-first cards
- Always show last changed by/time

## 8) Monetization
- Free: read-only + limited actions
- Pro: write actions, multi-account, advanced alerts
- Agency tier later: client workspace grouping

## 9) Success Metrics
- Action completion time <20s median
- WAU/MAU >40%
- Pro conversion >3%

## 10) Risks & Mitigations
- API change risk → robust adapter layer + versioned handlers
- Brand/compliance risk → avoid trademark misuse in positioning
- Wrong-action risk → dual confirmation + action logs

## 11) Build Plan
- Sprint 1: zones + DNS + cache purge
- Sprint 2: workers/pages status + alerting
- Sprint 3: hardening + billing + analytics

selected3.name: Tornado Tracker by TornadoPath
selected3.prd:
# Product Requirements Document — Tornado Tracker by TornadoPath

## 1) Product Summary
A high-clarity severe weather app focused on tornado-prone regions, delivering actionable risk alerts, map context, and family safety workflows.

## 2) User Problem
Generic weather apps are noisy and delayed for tornado-specific decisions. Users need location-prioritized alerts and simple “what to do now” guidance.

## 3) Target Users
- Households in tornado-risk areas
- Drivers/travelers crossing risk zones
- Volunteer emergency communities

## 4) Core Jobs-to-be-Done
- Know if my saved places are at risk now
- Get timely, trustworthy alert escalation
- Access immediate safety actions in one screen

## 5) MVP Scope
### 5.1 Location & Risk Model
- Multiple saved locations
- Risk score per location
- Geo-based alert radius tuning

### 5.2 Alerts
- Push alerts with severity level
- Escalation option (SMS/call in premium)
- Quiet hours + exception rules

### 5.3 Map & Timeline
- Live warning polygons
- 6-hour forecast timeline
- Event playback for context

### 5.4 Safety Mode
- Quick checklist
- Family status ping template
- Emergency contact shortcut

### 5.5 Trust Layer
- Source tags per event
- Last update timestamp
- Alert confidence indicators

## 6) Out of Scope (MVP)
- Full meteorological analytics workstation
- Regional social feed
- Hardware sensor integrations

## 7) UX Principles
- High contrast, low cognitive load
- Severity hierarchy always visible
- Fewer words, clearer actions

## 8) Monetization
- Free: core alerts + map
- Premium: advanced layers, family sharing, SMS escalation

## 9) Success Metrics
- Alert open rate >35%
- D30 retention in risk states >30%
- Premium conversion >2.5%

## 10) Risks & Mitigations
- False positive fatigue → confidence scoring and tuning controls
- Trust loss on stale data → explicit freshness and fallback sources
- Seasonality volatility → expand to broader severe weather modules later

## 11) Build Plan
- Sprint 1: location model + core alerts
- Sprint 2: map overlays + safety mode
- Sprint 3: premium escalation + reliability hardening
